target: AgentCore Gateway with CUSTOM_JWT authorizer (Cognito OIDC, M2M client_credentials)
note_account: real non-prod dev account id redacted to the 000000000000 placeholder per public-repo policy; created and exercised live on a non-prod Isengard dev account.
method: Live create + invoke against real AWS control/data planes. Created a Cognito user pool + hosted domain + resource server (custom scope sentinel-gw/invoke) + a confidential machine (client_credentials) app client; created a real AgentCore Gateway (protocolType=MCP, authorizerType=CUSTOM_JWT) whose customJWTAuthorizer.discoveryUrl points at the Cognito OIDC .well-known document and allowedClients pins the machine client id; minted a real RS256 access token via the client_credentials grant; then POSTed a JSON-RPC tools/list to the live gateway MCP endpoint three ways to prove enforcement.

resources.cognito_client_secret: managed server-side (GenerateSecret=true); value NEVER retrieved or printed
resources.gateway_target: cve-severity-tool (MCP Lambda target, GATEWAY_IAM_ROLE credential provider, status READY)

valid_jwt.meaning: authorized: gateway validated the RS256 token against the OIDC JWKS and served the MCP request

end_to_end_tool_call.note: Beyond auth enforcement: a real Lambda-backed MCP tool was attached as a gateway target (credentialProviderType=GATEWAY_IAM_ROLE) and invoked THROUGH the JWT gateway.
end_to_end_tool_call.meaning: The Cognito-authenticated caller listed the tool and executed it end-to-end; the Lambda ran server-side (gateway role invoked it) and returned a correct severity classification. This is the full roadmap intent: mint a Cognito token -> call a real tool through the CUSTOM_JWT gateway.

custom_jwt_authorization.summary: A live AgentCore Gateway enforces CUSTOM_JWT: a real Cognito-issued RS256 token is accepted (HTTP 200), while a missing token (401 'Missing Bearer token') and a garbage token (401 'Invalid Bearer token') are rejected. Enforcement is real, not asserted.

server_side_secret.summary: The confidential machine client's secret is managed server-side (GenerateSecret=true); its value was never retrieved or printed to console/logs, only used in-process to mint the token.

caveats: GA correction: AgentCore Gateway interceptorConfigurations are Lambda-based (interceptor.lambda.arn) with a separate policyEngineConfiguration (Bedrock guardrail policy engine, LOG_ONLY/ENFORCE); there is NO native 'Guardrail interceptor' primitive on CreateGateway. A guardrail is enforced either inside a Lambda interceptor (ApplyGuardrail) or via policyEngineConfiguration. This POC proves the CUSTOM_JWT authorization half live; the guardrail-redaction-on-a-gateway half is documented as the Lambda-interceptor/policy-engine path and is proven separately by the deployed Bedrock Guardrail evidence (live_verify_result.json: fake AWS secret BLOCKED, NAME/EMAIL ANONYMIZED via apply-guardrail).

The 200-vs-401 status split IS the authorization proof; the attached Lambda MCP target additionally proves an authenticated caller can execute a real tool end-to-end (tools/call -> Lambda -> correct severity), not merely list.
Cognito hosted-domain activation and IAM role propagation each required a short retry window before the token endpoint / CreateGateway succeeded — expected eventual-consistency, not a failure.
All identifiers scrubbed to <redacted>/000000000000 for the public repo; the run was on a non-prod dev account in us-east-1.
teardown: Gateway, Cognito app client, resource server, hosted domain, user pool, and the POC IAM role are torn down after evidence capture (POC-scoped, non-prod).
note: Real on-account proof that sentinel-harness's CUSTOM_JWT gateway story is live and enforced: a genuine Cognito OIDC provider + M2M client_credentials token is accepted by a real AgentCore Gateway while unauthenticated and malformed requests are rejected with 401. The authorizer binds to the OIDC discovery URL and pins allowedClients; the machine-client secret stays server-side.
